name: force_the_lock
description: Attempt to force a lock, quietly or otherwise. Reference specimen: exercises every key the custom-tool format understands. Copy it to Tools/force-the-lock.tool.json in any document store, then cut it down to what you actually need — every key below except name, description and outcomes is optional.

bonus.description: Skill bonus added to the score. Demonstrates: a number parameter, bounds, and a $param reference used as the roll's offset. Note the knock-on: at the default 0 the score tops out at exactly the ceiling, so the first outcome (> 100) only fires when a bonus is supplied — which is rather the point of a bonus. Run-time values are clamped into 0-10 before the roll ever sees them.

ceiling.description: Top of the difficulty scale. Demonstrates: a $param reference used as the roll's multiplier, so the 0-1 draw is scaled into 0-ceiling.

attempts.description: How many times you try. Demonstrates: an integer parameter (run-time values are rounded) and {{params.attempts}} in a message.

label.description: What you are forcing. Demonstrates: a string parameter. Strings cannot be referenced by $param — only numbers and integers can.

loudly.description: Whether you are past caring about the noise. Demonstrates: a boolean parameter. Booleans cannot be referenced by $param either.

outcomes.message: {{params.label}} does not so much open as capitulate. The score was {{value}} — past the ceiling entirely, which is what a bonus of {{params.bonus}} will do for a person. (Raw draw: {{roll}}.)
{{params.label}} clicks open on attempt {{params.attempts}}. Score {{value}}. Demonstrates an AND band: gte and lte in one object mean 70 <= value <= 100. There is no OR — order the table instead.
The tumblers settle on exactly {{value}}, which you choose to find significant. Demonstrates the eq comparator.
{{params.label}} shifts, then thinks better of it. Score {{value}}. Demonstrates neq composed with a band — 40 to 50, but never 42, which the outcome above already claimed.
Something inside {{params.label}} snaps, and it was not the lock. Score {{value}}. Demonstrates the lt comparator.
{{params.label}} holds, smugly. Score {{value}}. Demonstrates lte, and why order matters: this must sit AFTER lt:5, or lt:5 could never be reached.

{{params.label}} is giving way — slowly, and (loudly: {{params.loudly}}) not discreetly. Score {{value}} from a raw draw of {{roll}}. This is the mandatory catch-all: the LAST outcome must be `true`, and no earlier one may be, so every roll lands somewhere and nothing below is unreachable.
